turns.user_message: I think I’ve finally figured out that I’m just built to brace for disaster now. It only started a few weeks ago, but my chest already tightens before anything even happens. I catch myself holding my breath whenever the room gets quiet, waiting for some invisible shoe to drop. It’s exhausting to carry this heavy feeling around when nothing is actually wrong. I just keep telling myself to relax, but my body refuses to listen to my own logic.

I’ve come to accept that my whole system just refuses to dial itself down anymore. What used to be an occasional flutter has turned into a constant hum that follows me everywhere I go. I find myself scanning every corner for hidden threats, even when I’m completely alone in my own space. My shoulders are permanently locked up and my hands won’t stop fidgeting with anything they can find. It feels like I’m constantly running on a treadmill that keeps speeding up without my permission.

I know for a fact that I’m completely trapped inside my own head right now. Every single sound makes my heart slam against my ribs like it’s trying to break out. I can’t sit still, I can’t focus, and my breathing turns shallow the second I try to calm down. It’s like a sudden storm hits out of nowhere and leaves me gasping for air while my thoughts completely short circuit. I just feel completely overwhelmed by this heavy, suffocating pressure that won’t lift no matter what I do.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This just started recently.
